rules.message: Network call made structurally inside a for/for-of/for-in/while/do-while statement or an Array forEach/map/filter/reduce callback — verified against the parser's projected loop spans (the call's line falls within a loop statement's span, or within an array-iteration callback's own span), not merely co-occurring with loop syntax somewhere in the same function — so it fires one request per iteration, the HTTP analogue of N+1 (rate-limit / serial-latency risk). Batch via a bulk endpoint, or gather inputs and issue one request (or a bounded Promise.all). A file with no projected loop spans is silently skipped by this rule — for the `.ts`/`.tsx` files this rule scans, that realistically means a degraded parse (oversized file or syntax failure), the same graceful-degrade policy method-scan already applies to missing symbol spans. Suppress a vetted case with `// api-in-loop-ok`.
